title: The Gifts of the Holy Spirit
intro: The Spirit does not fill us and then leave us to ourselves. He equips us. He distributes gifts — not as rewards for the spiritually advanced, but as tools for the body of Christ. Each gift is an expression of the same Spirit, given for the same purpose: the common good.
reading:
1 Corinthians 12:4-11 lays out the diversity and the unity of spiritual gifts side by side: there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit. Varieties of service, but the same Lord. Varieties of activities, but the same God. The point is not the gifts themselves but the Giver — and the purpose, which is the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.

The gifts listed across 1 Corinthians 12, Romans 12:6-8, and other passages include wisdom, knowledge, faith, healing, miracles, prophecy, discernment, tongues, interpretation, teaching, exhortation, giving, leading, mercy, and more. No single list is exhaustive. The Spirit distributes as He wills.

1 Peter 4:10-11 frames the purpose of every gift: as each has received a gift, use it to serve one another, as good stewards of God's varied grace. Whoever serves, let him do so with the strength that God supplies — so that in everything God may be glorified through Jesus Christ.

Ephesians 4:12 makes the goal clear: the gifts are for building up the body of Christ. They are not for personal display. They are for the community.

You have been given something. The Spirit Himself placed it within you. The question is not whether you have a gift — it is whether you are using it for the One who gave it.
reflection: What gifts do you believe the Holy Spirit has placed in you? Are you using them to serve others, or have you been holding them back? What would it look like to steward them more intentionally?
abide: Holy Spirit, You have not left me empty-handed. You have placed gifts within me for the good of others and the glory of God. Give me the courage and humility to offer what You have given — not for my recognition, but for Your purposes.
